raw_consumer_statement:
I attempted to extend a flight with XXXX by phone on XX/XX/year> for {$280.00}, but they said the transaction didn't go through. I saw the charge on my credit card statement anyway. I never received a confirmation or ticket, and I never used the flight. XXXX now claims it's non-refundable and denies responsibility. My bank which is Citi is siding with the merchant despite no service being delivered. This is a clear case of being charged for failed service. 
I'm attaching a letter from XXXX acknowledging the charge and stating the ticket remains " open and unused, '' even though I had no access to it. This is a clear case of being charged for a service that was not provided. I had to purchase another flight with a different airline. 
I am requesting that the charge should be reversed or refunded as a matter of fair consumer protection.
